title: Create: Full Bloom
description: An alternative to grinding villager trades!
body:
Tired of constantly fighting with villagers for the end-game enchantments you need? Well tire no more! Full Bloom offers a create (and enchantment industry) integrated way to solve that problem! At the cost of some rocks, ancient flowers and your valued experience you can remedy just that!  

# Information
This mod adds two new items, the "Flosculus" and "Incomplete Flosculus," the Incomplete Flosculus is craftable with 4 Amethyst Shards, 2 Quartz, 2 Torchflowers and one valuable ingot/gem. Or findable in many different structures and fishing!
![The recipe for the flosculus, two torchflowers in the top left and bottom right corners, quartz in the middle of the top row and the end of the middle row, an iron ingot in the middle and amethyst shards everywhere else.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b56ecfd1d298504cfade3fb27249ea3437b31e63.png)

Right clicking the incomplete flosculus consumes 3 experience levels to create the full version!

Pottery sherds can be used together with the Flosculus in a smithing table on a book to create enchanted books! I tried to make them fit the sherd as best as possible! 

This is my first ever mod and more of an experiment with what is possible using fabric api! I made this in like 6 hours to please do ignore it's lack of polish! I wanted to make a mod with deals with my annoyance at getting max level enchants as minecraft's vanilla experience quite simply sucks. So instead of grinding villagers or mining quartz over and over only to risk those trips to the nether to rng, I decided on using two of minecraft's useless features (in terms of progression, I think they are aesthetically cool), Ancient flowers and Pottery Sherds!

# Requirements
Create mod is required to run this mod before 1.20.5, create mod makes it so that NBT data can be applied when crafting items, I am unsure as to how it is done and would like to make it so that create mod is not required, but for now, create mod is required.

Neoforge 1.21.1 Does not require create mod to work.

You have full permission to add this to your modpacks but cannot upload the mods unedited code anywhere else.
